text: <par>This is a contraction of the other Mawo <latinform>pʌtʃə̥</latinform> 'navel'.</par>
<par>This form may be a contraction of the another Qiang form, <latinform>wutʃl̀quote ə</latinform></par>
<par>=pus+blood, cf. Chinese nong-xie; Jingpo sai matsui.</par>

<par><latinform>pis</latinform>, <latinform>pies</latinform> are fused forms, with the disyllabic prototype shown in Yadu <latinform>pəʴ ʂən</latinform>. First element is said to be <latinform>piʁ</latinform>, but is more likely < <reconstruction>*pay</reconstruction> #51. (I think that this is 'pig' + 'flesh', which is the normal way to say 'meat' in Qiang. Sanlong Qiang also has something like <latinform>pəʴ ʂən </latinform>to mean 'body'. -- J.P. Evans)</par>

<par>This form bares a suspicious resemblance to Qiang (Mawo) <latinform>zda</latinform>, which has been assigned to <xref ref="1450">#1450</xref>. However, since the Qiangic languages have a strong tendency to develop the high front vowel <latinform>i</latinform> from PTB <latinform>*a</latinform>, a phenomenon which has been referred to as "brightening", these two Mawo forms may actually one and the same etymon.</par>
